config.description:
The exporter's runtime configuration. Each key maps to a DRM_EXPORTER_* (or
RUST_LOG) environment variable the binary reads; see the README for details.

devices.description: Restrict export to specific PCI slots, e.g. `["0000:03:00.0"]` (DRM_EXPORTER_DEVICES); empty exports every GPU discovered.
devices.title: devices

intervalSeconds.description: Seconds between GPU stat refreshes (DRM_EXPORTER_INTERVAL_SECONDS). Intel engine utilization is sampled across this window, so keep it at or below the Prometheus scrape interval.
intervalSeconds.title: intervalSeconds

logLevel.description: Log level (RUST_LOG): error, warn, info, debug, or trace.
logLevel.title: logLevel

port.description: Metrics HTTP listen port (DRM_EXPORTER_PORT); also the container and Service port.
port.title: port

config.title: config

daemonsetAnnotations.description: Annotations added to the DaemonSet (workload) metadata, e.g. `reloader.stakater.com/auto: "true"`.
daemonsetAnnotations.title: daemonsetAnnotations

dra.description:
Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA). On a cluster with a DRA GPU driver (e.g. the
Intel GPU resource driver, device class `gpu.intel.com`), the exporter can claim
GPU devices through a ResourceClaimTemplate instead of, or alongside, the
hostAccess mounts. The default is an admin-access *monitor* claim: read-only
visibility of every GPU on the node, allocated as `All` and not counted as
consumed (so it never steals a VF from a workload). Admin access requires the
release namespace to carry the label `resource.kubernetes.io/admin-access: "true"`.
Off by default; `resource.k8s.io/v1` is GA in Kubernetes 1.34.

adminAccess.description: Request admin access: read-only visibility of devices, not counted as consumed. Requires the namespace label `resource.kubernetes.io/admin-access: "true"`.
adminAccess.title: adminAccess

allocationMode.description: Allocation mode: `All` (every matching device on the node) or `ExactCount`.
allocationMode.title: allocationMode

apiVersion.description: ResourceClaimTemplate apiVersion; `resource.k8s.io/v1` is GA in Kubernetes 1.34. The rendered request uses the v1 `exactly` shape.
apiVersion.title: apiVersion

count.description: Device count, used only when allocationMode is `ExactCount`.
count.title: count

deviceClassName.description: DRA device class to request (the GPU driver's class), e.g. `gpu.intel.com` or `gpu.nvidia.com`.
deviceClassName.title: deviceClassName

enabled.description: Create a ResourceClaimTemplate and have the DaemonSet pods consume it (DRA-based GPU access).
enabled.title: enabled

requestName.description: Name of the device request within the claim.
requestName.title: requestName

tolerations.description: Device tolerations, letting the claim match tainted devices; the default tolerates the NoSchedule and NoExecute device taints (the monitor-claim pattern).
tolerations.title: tolerations

dra.title: dra

env.description: Extra environment variables for the container, as a map (templated).
env.title: env

envFrom.description: Sources of environment variables for the container (templated).
envFrom.title: envFrom

extraEnv.description:
TZ: UTC
Extra environment variables for the container, as a raw list (templated).
extraEnv.title: extraEnv

fullnameOverride.description: Override the generated name used for every resource's `metadata.name` (the chart "fullname").
fullnameOverride.title: fullnameOverride

global.description: Global values are values that can be accessed from any chart or subchart by exactly the same name.
global.title: global

hostAccess.description:
Host access. Reading GPU stats requires the node's DRM device nodes and sysfs;
Intel iGPU package temperature (and the RAPL power fallback) additionally need
the per-CPU MSR device nodes, which require the host `msr` kernel module (not
shipped by Talos — see the devCpu note below). Set a path to "" to skip mounting it.

devCpu.description: Host path for the per-CPU MSR nodes, mounted read-only, for Intel iGPU package temperature and the RAPL power fallback. Needs the host `msr` kernel module — autoloaded on most distros, but Talos does not ship it (siderolabs/extensions#620), so set this to "" on Talos (iGPU package temperature is then unavailable; engine/memory/frequency/power still work). Harmless (unused) on AMD GPUs; set to "" to skip.
devCpu.title: devCpu

devDri.description: Host path for the DRM device nodes, mounted read-write; required to discover and read GPUs.
devDri.title: devDri

hostNetwork.description: Use host networking (scrape via the node IP:port instead of through the Service).
hostNetwork.title: hostNetwork

sys.description: Host path for sysfs, mounted read-only; required for frequency, memory, AMD engine, and hwmon (temp/fan/power) stats.
sys.title: sys

hostAccess.title: hostAccess

digest.description: Pin the image by digest (sha256:…); set by the release pipeline. When set, it overrides the tag.
digest.title: digest

tag.description: Overrides the image tag; defaults to the chart appVersion.
tag.title: tag

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for private registries.
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

livenessProbe.description: Liveness probe. `/health` is the exporter's lightweight readiness endpoint (returns `OK`).
livenessProbe.title: livenessProbe

annotations.description: Annotations added to the dashboard ConfigMap (templated).
annotations.title: annotations

enabled.description: Render the Grafana dashboard ConfigMap (for the kube-prometheus-stack sidecar or grafana-operator).
enabled.title: enabled

allowCrossNamespaceImport.description: Allow a Grafana in any namespace to import this GrafanaDashboard.
allowCrossNamespaceImport.title: allowCrossNamespaceImport

enabled.description: Render a GrafanaDashboard CR (grafana-operator) referencing the ConfigMap, instead of relying on the sidecar label.
enabled.title: enabled

folder.description: Folder to create the dashboard in; empty uses the Grafana default.
folder.title: folder

matchLabels.description: Label selector matching the target Grafana instance (required when grafanaOperator is enabled).
matchLabels.title: matchLabels

resyncPeriod.description: Resync period for the operator to re-check the dashboard.
resyncPeriod.title: resyncPeriod

labels.description: Labels added to the dashboard ConfigMap.
labels.title: labels

namespace.description: Namespace for the dashboard objects; defaults to the release namespace.
namespace.title: namespace

enabled.description: Create a Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor (requires its CRDs).
enabled.title: enabled

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name used in the `app.kubernetes.io/name` label.
nameOverride.title: nameOverride

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for pod scheduling (templated). In mixed clusters, scope to GPU nodes — e.g. a Node Feature Discovery label like `feature.node.kubernetes.io/pci-0300_8086.present: "true"`. See the README.
nodeSelector.title: nodeSelector

podSecurityContext.description:
Pod-level securityContext. The exporter runs as root by default because GPU
telemetry needs MSR access (Intel package power) and the perf PMU on
locked-down kernels; see `securityContext` for the (narrow) capability set.
Override to run unprivileged where your nodes permit it.
podSecurityContext.title: podSecurityContext

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClass for the pod (templated); empty uses the cluster default.
priorityClassName.title: priorityClassName

securityContext.description:
Exporter container securityContext. Drops ALL capabilities, then adds only
what GPU telemetry needs: PERFMON (Intel i915/xe engine utilization via the
perf PMU) and SYS_RAWIO (Intel iGPU package temperature + the RAPL power
fallback via /dev/cpu/*/msr, which needs the host msr kernel module — absent on
Talos, so drop SYS_RAWIO there). AMD and all sysfs-based stats need neither.
Read-only root filesystem, no privilege escalation, not privileged.
securityContext.title: securityContext

automount.description: Mount the API token. The exporter never calls the Kubernetes API, so this is off by default.
automount.title: automount

name.description: ServiceAccount name; empty uses the chart fullname.
name.title: name

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Grace period for a clean shutdown.
terminationGracePeriodSeconds.title: terminationGracePeriodSeconds

repository.description: `helm test` connection-pod image; a gcr-mirrored curl, so the test never pulls from Docker Hub.
repository.title: repository

volumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts on the exporter container (templated).
volumeMounts.title: volumeMounts

volumes.description: Additional volumes on the pod, beyond the host mounts (templated).
volumes.title: volumes
